← all 386 games

Memory Test Games

How many digits can you actually hold in your head? The classic answer is 7±2 — these games let you test it. Sequences, positions, patterns and numbers, each with a hard score at the end that tells you exactly where you stand. Fair warning: they start easy on purpose.

VIRAL

Memory Nine

repeat the growing sequence

hold 9 steps
VIRAL

Story Expire

stories 24h FOMO

beat the rings record
VIRAL

'only 90s kids remember' nostalgia-chain posts that bait recall of childhood sequences

FLIP PAIRS

nostalgia-bait photo pairs ('here vs now') that farm the match-recognition reflex

VIRAL

'before and after' transformation clips that train the eye to hunt for what changed

hidden-object / 'what\'s different' clip format that parks the pattern in short-term memory

'name it if you remember it' challenge posts that weaponize the recall reflex for shares

STORY SEQUENCE

CALM

ECHO FADE

nostalgia-ASMR 'remember this sound?' posts that park a memory and reopen it gently

no score. just you.
CALM

RECALL GARDEN

'on this day' memory-bait posts that pull the nostalgia reflex into a feed loop

no score. just you.

Questions

What is a normal short-term memory score?

Most people hold 5 to 9 items in short-term memory (the classic “seven plus or minus two”). The number games here will find your personal limit in about two minutes.

Do memory games improve memory?

They reliably improve at the game itself; broader transfer is debated in research. Either way, they are a fun benchmark — and the scores are very shareable.
